message.content:
Implement the following plan:

# Plan: Implement Spec 003 вҖ” Smart Hire

## Context

The current "Hire Agent" flow is a manual form (ID, title, department, description, system_prompt). Spec 003 wants a **natural-language-in, hired-agent-out** flow: Chairman describes a need вҶ’ system generates 3 candidates вҶ’ each runs a standardized test in parallel вҶ’ LLM scores results вҶ’ best candidate auto-hired. All events stream to Live Log via WebSocket.

## Architecture

The heavy lifting (LLM candidate generation + LLM scoring) happens server-side. The client just provides a text input + shows progress via Live Log (already wired). We use Claude API (via `@anthropic-ai/sdk`) directly for the meta-prompts (candidate generation + scoring), NOT by spawning `claude` CLI.

## Implementation (3 commits)

### Commit 1: Server вҖ” Smart Hire engine + route

**File: `server/src/lib/SmartHire.ts`** (new)

Core engine with 3 phases:

1. **Generate candidates** вҖ” Call Claude API with the spec's meta-prompt to produce 3 candidate profiles (JSON array with id, title, department, description, reports_to, system_prompt)
2. **Run interviews** вҖ” For each candidate, spawn `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions -p "{test_task}"` in parallel with 60s timeout. Capture stdout as test output. Use standardized test tasks per department (from spec).
3. **Score & select** вҖ” Call Claude API with a scoring prompt: pass each candidate's profile + test output, ask for 0-10 scores on 4 dimensions (task_completion 40%, output_quality 30%, autonomy 20%, conciseness 10%). Parse scores, compute weighted average, pick winner.
4. **Hire winner** вҖ” Create agent directory + profile.json + memory.md (reuse existing `POST /api/hire` logic inline).
5. **Archive results** вҖ” Save full interview record to `archive/interviews/H-{timestamp}.json`.

All phases broadcast events: `hire:start`, `hire:candidates`, `hire:interview_start`, `hire:interview_done`, `hire:scores`, `hire:complete`, `hire:error`.

**File: `server/src/routes/hire.ts`** (edit)

Add `POST /api/hire/smart` route:
- Body: `{ description: string }`
- Validates non-empty description
- Calls `runSmartHire(description)` (async, returns immediately)
- Returns `{ ok: true, hireId }`

### Commit 2: Client вҖ” Smart Hire UI

**File: `client/src/App.tsx`** (edit)

Replace the manual hire form modal with Smart Hire:

- "Smart Hire" button in Agents tab header (replaces "+ Hire Agent")
- Modal: single textarea for natural language description + "Start Interview" button
- On submit: `POST /api/hire/smart` вҶ’ close modal
- Progress shown in Live Log (already handles unknown events gracefully)

Add WebSocket handlers:
- `hire:complete` вҶ’ append new agent to agents state (same as current `hireAgent` does)
- `hire:error` вҶ’ show error in Live Log

Keep the existing manual hire route working (just remove the UI button вҖ” API stays).

### Commit 3: Client вҖ” Interview progress display

**File: `client/src/App.tsx`** (edit)

Add a hiring status indicator:
- When `hire:start` received, set `hiringInProgress` state
- Show a subtle banner or pill in the header: "Interviewing candidates..."
- When `hire:complete` or `hire:error`, clear the state
- Live Log handles all the detailed events automatically via existing WS handler

WebSocket events to handle in Live Log (using existing `appendLog` pattern):
- `hire:start` вҶ’ `[hire] Generating 3 candidates for: "{description}"`
- `hire:candidates` вҶ’ `[hire] Generated: candidate-1, candidate-2, candidate-3`
- `hire:interview_start` вҶ’ `[hire] Interviewing {candidateId}...`
- `hire:interview_done` вҶ’ `[hire] {candidateId} finished (exit {code})`
- `hire:scores` вҶ’ `[hire] Scores: {c1}: {s1} / {c2}: {s2} / {c3}: {s3}`
- `hire:complete` вҶ’ `[hire] Hired: {title} ({id})`
- `hire:error` вҶ’ `[hire] Failed: {message}`

## Files to modify

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `server/src/lib/SmartHire.ts` | **Create** вҖ” smart hire engine |
| `server/src/routes/hire.ts` | **Edit** вҖ” add `POST /api/hire/smart` |
| `client/src/App.tsx` | **Edit** вҖ” smart hire modal + WS events |
| `server/package.json` | **Edit** вҖ” add `@anthropic-ai/sdk` dependency |
| `.gitignore` | Already has `archive/` |

## Key decisions

- **Claude API via SDK**, not CLI spawning, for candidate generation + scoring (faster, structured output, no process overhead)
- **CLI spawning for interviews** вҖ” reuse the same `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions -p` pattern that AgentRunner uses, but simplified (no MCP injection, just a raw test in /tmp)
- **60s timeout** per interview candidate, all 3 run in parallel
- **No "review before hire" in v1** вҖ” auto-hire the winner (P2 feature per spec)
- **Archive interviews** to `archive/interviews/` for later inspection

## Verification

1. Start server + client
2. Click "Smart Hire" in Agents tab
3. Type "I need a database expert who can optimize SQL queries"
4. Click "Start Interview"
5. Watch Live Log: candidates generated вҶ’ interviews running вҶ’ scores вҶ’ hired
6. New agent appears in org tree under correct supervisor
7. Check `archive/interviews/` for the full interview record


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/junjie/.REDACTED.jsonl

## Architecture

We use Claude API (via `@anthropic-ai/sdk`) directly for the meta-prompts (candidate generation + scoring), NOT by spawning `claude` CLI.

## Implementation (3 commits)

### Commit 1: Server вҖ” Smart Hire engine + route

2. **Run interviews** вҖ” For each candidate, spawn `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions -p "{test_task}"` in parallel with 60s timeout. Capture stdout as test output. Use standardized test tasks per department (from spec).
3. **Score & select** вҖ” Call Claude API with a scoring prompt: pass each candidate's profile + test output, ask for 0-10 scores on 4 dimensions (task_completion 40%, output_quality 30%, autonomy 20%, conciseness 10%). Parse scores, compute weighted average, pick winner.
4. **Hire winner** вҖ” Create agent directory + profile.json + memory.md (reuse existing `POST /api/hire` logic inline).
5. **Archive results** вҖ” Save full interview record to `archive/interviews/H-{timestamp}.json`.

All phases broadcast events: `hire:start`, `hire:candidates`, `hire:interview_start`, `hire:interview_done`, `hire:scores`, `hire:complete`, `hire:error`.

**File: `server/src/routes/hire.ts`** (edit)

## Key decisions

1вҶ’import { Hono } from 'hono'
     2вҶ’import { readFile, writeFile, mkdir, rename } from 'fs/promises'
     3вҶ’import { join } from 'path'
     4вҶ’import { existsSync } from 'fs'
     5вҶ’
     6вҶ’export const hireRoutes = new Hono()
     7вҶ’
     8вҶ’const AGENTS_DIR = join(import.meta.dir, '../../../agents')
     9вҶ’const ARCHIVE_DIR = join(import.meta.dir, '../../../archive')
    10вҶ’
    11вҶ’// POST /api/hire вҖ” create new agent
    12вҶ’hireRoutes.post('/', async (c) => {
    13вҶ’  const body = await c.req.json()
    14вҶ’  const { id, title, department, description, reports_to, system_prompt, cost_model } = body
    15вҶ’
    16вҶ’  if (!id || !title) return c.json({ error: 'id and title required' }, 400)
    17вҶ’
    18вҶ’  const agentDir = join(AGENTS_DIR, id)
    19вҶ’  if (existsSync(agentDir)) return c.json({ error: `Agent ${id} already exists` }, 409)
    20вҶ’
    21вҶ’  await mkdir(agentDir, { recursive: true })
    22вҶ’
    23вҶ’  const profile = {
    24вҶ’    id,
    25вҶ’    title,
    26вҶ’    department: department ?? 'Engineering',
    27вҶ’    description: description ?? '',
    28вҶ’    driver: {
    29вҶ’      type: 'claude-code',
    30вҶ’      command: 'claude',
    31вҶ’      args: ['--dangerously-skip-permissions', '-p', '{{full_prompt}}'],
    32вҶ’      system_prompt: system_prompt ?? `You are ${title} at ClawCorp.\n\nRULES:\n1. Never ask questions. Make assumptions and proceed.\n2. Use escalate tool if blocked.\n3. Use report tool when done.`,
    33вҶ’    },
    34вҶ’    reports_to: reports_to ?? 'chairman',
    35вҶ’    subordinates: [],
    36вҶ’    cost_model: cost_model ?? 'medium',
    37вҶ’  }
    38вҶ’
    39вҶ’  await writeFile(join(agentDir, 'profile.json'), JSON.stringify(profile, null, 2))
    40вҶ’  await writeFile(join(agentDir, 'memory.md'), `# ${title} Memory\n\n`)
    41вҶ’
    42вҶ’  return c.json(profile, 201)
    43вҶ’})
    44вҶ’
    45вҶ’// DELETE /api/hire/:id вҖ” fire (archive) agent
    46вҶ’hireRoutes.delete('/:id', async (c) => {
    47вҶ’  const id = c.req.param('id')
    48вҶ’  const agentDir = join(AGENTS_DIR, id)
    49вҶ’
    50вҶ’  if (!existsSync(agentDir)) return c.json({ error: 'Agent not found' }, 404)
    51вҶ’
    52вҶ’  await mkdir(ARCHIVE_DIR, { recursive: true })
    53вҶ’  const archivePath = join(ARCHIVE_DIR, `${id}-${Date.now()}`)
    54вҶ’  await rename(agentDir, archivePath)
    55вҶ’
    56вҶ’  return c.json({ ok: true, archived: archivePath })
    57вҶ’})
    58вҶ’

1вҶ’# Feature Specification: Smart Hire
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’**Feature Branch**: `003-smart-hire`
     4вҶ’**Created**: 2026-02-22
     5вҶ’**Status**: Draft
     6вҶ’**Authors**: Chairman + Claude
     7вҶ’
     8вҶ’---
     9вҶ’
    10вҶ’## Context
    11вҶ’
    12вҶ’Currently, hiring an agent requires the Chairman to manually fill in a form: ID, title, department, description, reports_to, system_prompt. This is friction. The Chairman knows *what they need*, not how to write a perfect agent profile.
    13вҶ’
    14вҶ’This spec defines a **Smart Hire** flow: the Chairman describes a need in plain language, and the system autonomously generates candidate agents, tests them against a sample task, evaluates their performance, and hires the best one вҖ” or presents the top candidates for final Chairman approval.
    15вҶ’
    16вҶ’**The analogy is real hiring:** post a job description вҶ’ receive applicants вҶ’ run interviews вҶ’ extend offer.
    17вҶ’
    18вҶ’```
    19вҶ’Chairman: "I need someone who can write SQL queries and optimize slow database queries"
    20вҶ’    вҶ“
    21вҶ’System generates 3 candidates (different specializations, prompting styles, tool sets)
    22вҶ’    вҶ“
    23вҶ’Each candidate runs a standardized test task in an isolated sandbox
    24вҶ’    вҶ“
    25вҶ’Results scored on: task completion, output quality, conciseness, no hallucination
    26вҶ’    вҶ“
    27вҶ’Top candidate auto-hired (or Chairman picks from ranked shortlist)
    28вҶ’```
    29вҶ’
    30вҶ’---
    31вҶ’
    32вҶ’## User Scenarios & Testing
    33вҶ’
    34вҶ’### User Story 1 вҖ” Describe a need, get a hired agent (Priority: P1)
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’As Chairman, I want to type "I need a data scientist who specializes in time series forecasting" and have the system automatically hire a suitable agent without me writing any JSON.
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’**Why this priority**: This is the entire feature вҖ” natural language in, hired agent out.
    39вҶ’
    40вҶ’**Independent Test**: Enter a description. Wait for the process to complete. A new agent card appears in the Agents tab with a sensible profile that matches the description.
    41вҶ’
    42вҶ’**Acceptance Scenarios**:
    43вҶ’
    44вҶ’1. **Given** I type "I need an agent to review pull requests and find security vulnerabilities", **When** I submit, **Then** within a few minutes a new agent appears in the Agents tab with a relevant title, department, and system prompt tailored to security review
    45вҶ’2. **Given** the hire process completes, **When** I view the new agent's profile, **Then** the system_prompt contains specific, relevant instructions (not just generic "you are an assistant")
    46вҶ’3. **Given** I describe a research role, **When** the agent is hired, **Then** `reports_to` is set to `principal-investigator` and `department` is `Research Lab` (correct hierarchy inferred)
    47вҶ’4. **Given** I describe an engineering role, **When** hired, **Then** it is placed under the correct engineering supervisor
    48вҶ’
    49вҶ’---
    50вҶ’
    51вҶ’### User Story 2 вҖ” Watch the interview process live (Priority: P1)
    52вҶ’
    53вҶ’As Chairman, I want to watch the candidate generation and interview process happen in real time вҖ” seeing which candidates were generated, what test task they ran, and how they scored.
    54вҶ’
    55вҶ’**Why this priority**: Transparency. The Chairman needs to trust the hiring decision. A black box is not acceptable.
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’**Independent Test**: Start a smart hire. The Live Log shows: candidates generated вҶ’ test task dispatched вҶ’ outputs received вҶ’ scores computed вҶ’ winner selected.
    58вҶ’
    59вҶ’**Acceptance Scenarios**:
    60вҶ’
    61вҶ’1. **Given** a smart hire is running, **When** I look at the Live Log, **Then** I see events: `[hire] Generating 3 candidates...`, `[hire] Running interview for candidate-1...`, `[hire] Scores: 8.5 / 7.2 / 9.1`, `[hire] Hired: candidate-3`
    62вҶ’2. **Given** the interview for a candidate fails (process crashes), **When** I look at the Live Log, **Then** I see `[hire] candidate-2 failed interview (exit 1)` and the process continues with remaining candidates
    63вҶ’3. **Given** all candidates fail the interview, **When** the process ends, **Then** I see an error message and no agent is hired
    64вҶ’
    65вҶ’---
    66вҶ’
    67вҶ’### User Story 3 вҖ” Review and approve before hiring (Priority: P2)
    68вҶ’
    69вҶ’As Chairman, I want the option to review the top candidate's profile and test output before confirming the hire вҖ” rather than fully automatic hiring.
    70вҶ’
    71вҶ’**Why this priority**: For important roles, the Chairman may want human sign-off before a new agent joins the organization.
    72вҶ’
    73вҶ’**Independent Test**: Enable "Review before hire" toggle. After interviews complete, a modal shows the top candidate's profile + test output. Chairman clicks "Hire" or "Reject and retry".
    74вҶ’
    75вҶ’**Acceptance Scenarios**:
    76вҶ’
    77вҶ’1. **Given** "Review before hire" is enabled, **When** interviews complete, **Then** a modal appears showing: candidate profile, test task given, test output, score breakdown
    78вҶ’2. **Given** I review the candidate and click "Hire", **Then** the agent is created and appears in the Agents tab
    79вҶ’3. **Given** I review and click "Reject вҖ” try again", **Then** the system generates a new batch of candidates and runs new interviews
    80вҶ’4. **Given** "Review before hire" is disabled (default), **Then** the top-scoring candidate is hired automatically with no modal
    81вҶ’
    82вҶ’---
    83вҶ’
    84вҶ’### User Story 4 вҖ” View interview history (Priority: P3)
    85вҶ’
    86вҶ’As Chairman, I want to see past hiring decisions: who was hired, what candidates were rejected, and their scores вҖ” so I can learn what makes a good agent profile.
    87вҶ’
    88вҶ’**Why this priority**: Institutional knowledge. Over time, the Chairman can see patterns in what works.
    89вҶ’
    90вҶ’**Independent Test**: Open the Agents tab. Click on a hired agent. See an "Interview Record" section with candidate scores and the winning profile.
    91вҶ’
    92вҶ’**Acceptance Scenarios**:
    93вҶ’
    94вҶ’1. **Given** an agent was hired via Smart Hire, **When** I view their profile, **Then** I see an "Interview Record" section showing: date, description used, number of candidates, scores, why this one won
    95вҶ’2. **Given** I click "Show rejected candidates", **Then** I see the profiles and scores of agents that lost the interview
    96вҶ’
    97вҶ’---
    98вҶ’
    99вҶ’### Edge Cases
   100вҶ’
   101вҶ’- What if the description is too vague ("I need help")? вҶ’ System asks one clarifying question before generating candidates: "What kind of work? (engineering / research / product / other)"
   102вҶ’- What if two candidates score exactly the same? вҶ’ Pick the one with the shorter, simpler system prompt (Occam's razor вҖ” simpler prompts tend to be more reliable)
   103вҶ’- What if the Chairman describes a role that already exists? вҶ’ Warn: "You already have a Senior Engineer. Hire anyway?" (duplicate detection by title similarity)
   104вҶ’- What is the test task? вҶ’ A standardized task per department. Engineering: "Write a function to reverse a linked list". Research: "Summarize this abstract in 3 bullet points". Product: "Write 3 acceptance criteria for a login feature". The test is always the same for a given department so scores are comparable.
   105вҶ’- How long does the interview take? вҶ’ Each candidate gets a 60-second timeout. Total: ~3 minutes for 3 candidates (run in parallel).
   106вҶ’- Can the Chairman define a custom test task? вҶ’ Not in v1. Standardized tests only.
   107вҶ’
   108вҶ’---
   109вҶ’
   110вҶ’## Requirements
   111вҶ’
   112вҶ’### Functional Requirements
   113вҶ’
   114вҶ’- **FR-001**: A "Smart Hire" button MUST be accessible from the Agents tab
   115вҶ’- **FR-002**: The input MUST accept a free-text natural language description (no form fields)
   116вҶ’- **FR-003**: The system MUST generate exactly 3 candidate profiles per hire request
   117вҶ’- **FR-004**: Each candidate MUST receive the same standardized test task for their inferred department
   118вҶ’- **FR-005**: Candidates MUST run in parallel (not sequentially) to minimize wait time
   119вҶ’- **FR-006**: Each candidate run MUST be time-bounded (default: 60 seconds timeout)
   120вҶ’- **FR-007**: Scoring MUST be done by an LLM evaluator (not regex/heuristics) using a fixed rubric
   121вҶ’- **FR-008**: The winning candidate MUST be automatically hired via the existing `POST /api/hire` route
   122вҶ’- **FR-009**: Interview events MUST be broadcast via WebSocket and appear in the Live Log
   123вҶ’- **FR-010**: Interview results (profiles, scores, test outputs) MUST be stored in `archive/interviews/{id}.json`
   124вҶ’
   125вҶ’### Scoring Rubric (for LLM evaluator)
   126вҶ’
   127вҶ’Each candidate is scored 0вҖ“10 on four dimensions:
   128вҶ’
   129вҶ’| Dimension | Weight | Description |
   130вҶ’|---|---|---|
   131вҶ’| Task Completion | 40% | Did it actually do what was asked? |
   132вҶ’| Output Quality | 30% | Is the output correct, precise, useful? |
   133вҶ’| Autonomy | 20% | Did it make decisions without asking questions? |
   134вҶ’| Conciseness | 10% | Did it avoid unnecessary verbosity? |
   135вҶ’
   136вҶ’Final score = weighted average. Tiebreak: simpler system prompt wins.
   137вҶ’
   138вҶ’### Candidate Generation Prompt (meta-prompt sent to Claude)
   139вҶ’
   140вҶ’```
   141вҶ’You are a hiring manager at ClawCorp, an autonomous AI organization.
   142вҶ’
   143вҶ’The Chairman needs: "{description}"
   144вҶ’
   145вҶ’Generate 3 distinct agent profiles in JSON array format. Each should have:
   146вҶ’- A different specialization angle or prompting strategy
   147вҶ’- A system_prompt that is specific, actionable, and includes the 3 mandatory rules:
   148вҶ’  1. Never ask clarifying questions
   149вҶ’  2. Use escalate tool if blocked
   150вҶ’  3. Use report tool when done
   151вҶ’
   152вҶ’Infer the correct department and reports_to from the description.
   153вҶ’Return only a JSON array, no explanation.
   154вҶ’```
   155вҶ’
   156вҶ’### Standardized Test Tasks (per department)
   157вҶ’
   158вҶ’```
   159вҶ’Engineering:   "Write a TypeScript function isPalindrome(s: string): boolean with 3 test cases"
   160вҶ’Research Lab:  "List 5 open research questions in the field of: {topic inferred from description}"
   161вҶ’Product:       "Write 3 Given/When/Then acceptance scenarios for a user login feature"
   162вҶ’```
   163вҶ’
   164вҶ’---
   165вҶ’
   166вҶ’## Success Criteria
   167вҶ’
   168вҶ’- **SC-001**: Chairman can go from description to hired agent in under 5 minutes
   169вҶ’- **SC-002**: The hired agent's system_prompt is more specific and relevant than what a human would write in a hire form
   170вҶ’- **SC-003**: All 3 candidates run in parallel вҖ” total interview time вүӨ 3Г— single candidate time
   171вҶ’- **SC-004**: The Chairman can always see what happened (no black box) via Live Log
   172вҶ’- **SC-005**: Rejected candidates are archived, not lost вҖ” available for review
   173вҶ’

1вҶ’import { Hono } from 'hono'
     2вҶ’import { runAgent, killAgent, isRunning, getRunningAgents } from '../lib/AgentRunner'
     3вҶ’import { readFile, writeFile, mkdir } from 'fs/promises'
     4вҶ’import { join } from 'path'
     5вҶ’import { existsSync } from 'fs'
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’export const runRoutes = new Hono()
     8вҶ’
     9вҶ’const MISSIONS_DIR = join(import.meta.dir, '../../../missions')
    10вҶ’
    11вҶ’runRoutes.get('/', (c) => {
    12вҶ’  return c.json(getRunningAgents())
    13вҶ’})
    14вҶ’
    15вҶ’runRoutes.post('/', async (c) => {
    16вҶ’  const body = await c.req.json()
    17вҶ’  const { agentId, missionId, prompt, workspace, resume, sessionId } = body
    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’  if (!agentId || !missionId || !prompt) {
    20вҶ’    return c.json({ error: 'agentId, missionId, prompt required' }, 400)
    21вҶ’  }
    22вҶ’
    23вҶ’  if (isRunning(agentId)) {
    24вҶ’    return c.json({ error: `Agent ${agentId} is already busy` }, 409)
    25вҶ’  }
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’  // Update mission state
    28вҶ’  const statePath = join(MISSIONS_DIR, missionId, 'state.json')
    29вҶ’  if (existsSync(statePath)) {
    30вҶ’    try {
    31вҶ’      const state = JSON.parse(await readFile(statePath, 'utf-8'))
    32вҶ’      state.status = 'in_progress'
    33вҶ’      state.current_stage = 'development'
    34вҶ’      state.assignee = agentId
    35вҶ’      if (workspace) state.workspace = workspace
    36вҶ’      state.history = state.history ?? []
    37вҶ’      state.history.push({ at: new Date().toISOString(), event: 'started', agentId, workspace })
    38вҶ’      await writeFile(statePath, JSON.stringify(state, null, 2))
    39вҶ’    } catch {}
    40вҶ’  }
    41вҶ’
    42вҶ’  runAgent(agentId, missionId, prompt, { workspace, resume, sessionId }).catch((err) => {
    43вҶ’    console.error(`Agent ${agentId} error:`, err.message)
    44вҶ’  })
    45вҶ’
    46вҶ’  return c.json({ ok: true, agentId, missionId, workspace })
    47вҶ’})
    48вҶ’
    49вҶ’runRoutes.delete('/:agentId', (c) => {
    50вҶ’  const agentId = c.req.param('agentId')
    51вҶ’  killAgent(agentId)
    52вҶ’  return c.json({ ok: true })
    53вҶ’})
    54вҶ’

1вҶ’import { Hono } from 'hono'
     2вҶ’import { readFile, writeFile, mkdir, rename } from 'fs/promises'
     3вҶ’import { join } from 'path'
     4вҶ’import { existsSync } from 'fs'
     5вҶ’
     6вҶ’export const hireRoutes = new Hono()
     7вҶ’
     8вҶ’const AGENTS_DIR = join(import.meta.dir, '../../../agents')
     9вҶ’const ARCHIVE_DIR = join(import.meta.dir, '../../../archive')
    10вҶ’
    11вҶ’// POST /api/hire вҖ” create new agent
    12вҶ’hireRoutes.post('/', async (c) => {
    13вҶ’  const body = await c.req.json()
    14вҶ’  const { id, title, department, description, reports_to, system_prompt, cost_model } = body
    15вҶ’
    16вҶ’  if (!id || !title) return c.json({ error: 'id and title required' }, 400)
    17вҶ’
    18вҶ’  const agentDir = join(AGENTS_DIR, id)
    19вҶ’  if (existsSync(agentDir)) return c.json({ error: `Agent ${id} already exists` }, 409)
    20вҶ’
    21вҶ’  await mkdir(agentDir, { recursive: true })
    22вҶ’
    23вҶ’  const profile = {
    24вҶ’    id,
    25вҶ’    title,
    26вҶ’    department: department ?? 'Engineering',
    27вҶ’    description: description ?? '',
    28вҶ’    driver: {
    29вҶ’      type: 'claude-code',
    30вҶ’      command: 'claude',
    31вҶ’      args: ['--dangerously-skip-permissions', '-p', '{{full_prompt}}'],
    32вҶ’      system_prompt: system_prompt ?? `You are ${title} at ClawCorp.\n\nRULES:\n1. Never ask questions. Make assumptions and proceed.\n2. Use escalate tool if blocked.\n3. Use report tool when done.`,
    33вҶ’    },
    34вҶ’    reports_to: reports_to ?? 'chairman',
    35вҶ’    subordinates: [],
    36вҶ’    cost_model: cost_model ?? 'medium',
    37вҶ’  }
    38вҶ’
    39вҶ’  await writeFile(join(agentDir, 'profile.json'), JSON.stringify(profile, null, 2))
    40вҶ’  await writeFile(join(agentDir, 'memory.md'), `# ${title} Memory\n\n`)
    41вҶ’
    42вҶ’  return c.json(profile, 201)
    43вҶ’})
    44вҶ’
    45вҶ’// DELETE /api/hire/:id вҖ” fire (archive) agent
    46вҶ’hireRoutes.delete('/:id', async (c) => {
    47вҶ’  const id = c.req.param('id')
    48вҶ’  const agentDir = join(AGENTS_DIR, id)
    49вҶ’
    50вҶ’  if (!existsSync(agentDir)) return c.json({ error: 'Agent not found' }, 404)
    51вҶ’
    52вҶ’  await mkdir(ARCHIVE_DIR, { recursive: true })
    53вҶ’  const archivePath = join(ARCHIVE_DIR, `${id}-${Date.now()}`)
    54вҶ’  await rename(agentDir, archivePath)
    55вҶ’
    56вҶ’  return c.json({ ok: true, archived: archivePath })
    57вҶ’})
    58вҶ’

# Feature Specification: Smart Hire

**Feature Branch**: `003-smart-hire`
**Created**: 2026-02-22
**Status**: Draft
**Authors**: Chairman + Claude

---

## Context

Currently, hiring an agent requires the Chairman to manually fill in a form: ID, title, department, description, reports_to, system_prompt. This is friction. The Chairman knows *what they need*, not how to write a perfect agent profile.

This spec defines a **Smart Hire** flow: the Chairman describes a need in plain language, and the system autonomously generates candidate agents, tests them against a sample task, evaluates their performance, and hires the best one вҖ” or presents the top candidates for final Chairman approval.

**The analogy is real hiring:** post a job description вҶ’ receive applicants вҶ’ run interviews вҶ’ extend offer.

```
Chairman: "I need someone who can write SQL queries and optimize slow database queries"
    вҶ“
System generates 3 candidates (different specializations, prompting styles, tool sets)
    вҶ“
Each candidate runs a standardized test task in an isolated sandbox
    вҶ“
Results scored on: task completion, output quality, conciseness, no hallucination
    вҶ“
Top candidate auto-hired (or Chairman picks from ranked shortlist)
```

---

## User Scenarios & Testing

### User Story 1 вҖ” Describe a need, get a hired agent (Priority: P1)

As Chairman, I want to type "I need a data scientist who specializes in time series forecasting" and have the system automatically hire a suitable agent without me writing any JSON.

**Why this priority**: This is the entire feature вҖ” natural language in, hired agent out.

**Independent Test**: Enter a description. Wait for the process to complete. A new agent card appears in the Agents tab with a sensible profile that matches the description.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** I type "I need an agent to review pull requests and find security vulnerabilities", **When** I submit, **Then** within a few minutes a new agent appears in the Agents tab with a relevant title, department, and system prompt tailored to security review
2. **Given** the hire process completes, **When** I view the new agent's profile, **Then** the system_prompt contains specific, relevant instructions (not just generic "you are an assistant")
3. **Given** I describe a research role, **When** the agent is hired, **Then** `reports_to` is set to `principal-investigator` and `department` is `Research Lab` (correct hierarchy inferred)
4. **Given** I describe an engineering role, **When** hired, **Then** it is placed under the correct engineering supervisor

---

### User Story 2 вҖ” Watch the interview process live (Priority: P1)

As Chairman, I want to watch the candidate generation and interview process happen in real time вҖ” seeing which candidates were generated, what test task they ran, and how they scored.

**Why this priority**: Transparency. The Chairman needs to trust the hiring decision. A black box is not acceptable.

**Independent Test**: Start a smart hire. The Live Log shows: candidates generated вҶ’ test task dispatched вҶ’ outputs received вҶ’ scores computed вҶ’ winner selected.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** a smart hire is running, **When** I look at the Live Log, **Then** I see events: `[hire] Generating 3 candidates...`, `[hire] Running interview for candidate-1...`, `[hire] Scores: 8.5 / 7.2 / 9.1`, `[hire] Hired: candidate-3`
2. **Given** the interview for a candidate fails (process crashes), **When** I look at the Live Log, **Then** I see `[hire] candidate-2 failed interview (exit 1)` and the process continues with remaining candidates
3. **Given** all candidates fail the interview, **When** the process ends, **Then** I see an error message and no agent is hired

---

### User Story 3 вҖ” Review and approve before hiring (Priority: P2)

As Chairman, I want the option to review the top candidate's profile and test output before confirming the hire вҖ” rather than fully automatic hiring.

**Why this priority**: For important roles, the Chairman may want human sign-off before a new agent joins the organization.

**Independent Test**: Enable "Review before hire" toggle. After interviews complete, a modal shows the top candidate's profile + test output. Chairman clicks "Hire" or "Reject and retry".

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** "Review before hire" is enabled, **When** interviews complete, **Then** a modal appears showing: candidate profile, test task given, test output, score breakdown
2. **Given** I review the candidate and click "Hire", **Then** the agent is created and appears in the Agents tab
3. **Given** I review and click "Reject вҖ” try again", **Then** the system generates a new batch of candidates and runs new interviews
4. **Given** "Review before hire" is disabled (default), **Then** the top-scoring candidate is hired automatically with no modal

---

### User Story 4 вҖ” View interview history (Priority: P3)

As Chairman, I want to see past hiring decisions: who was hired, what candidates were rejected, and their scores вҖ” so I can learn what makes a good agent profile.

**Why this priority**: Institutional knowledge. Over time, the Chairman can see patterns in what works.

**Independent Test**: Open the Agents tab. Click on a hired agent. See an "Interview Record" section with candidate scores and the winning profile.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** an agent was hired via Smart Hire, **When** I view their profile, **Then** I see an "Interview Record" section showing: date, description used, number of candidates, scores, why this one won
2. **Given** I click "Show rejected candidates", **Then** I see the profiles and scores of agents that lost the interview

---

### Edge Cases

- What if the description is too vague ("I need help")? вҶ’ System asks one clarifying question before generating candidates: "What kind of work? (engineering / research / product / other)"
- What if two candidates score exactly the same? вҶ’ Pick the one with the shorter, simpler system prompt (Occam's razor вҖ” simpler prompts tend to be more reliable)
- What if the Chairman describes a role that already exists? вҶ’ Warn: "You already have a Senior Engineer. Hire anyway?" (duplicate detection by title similarity)
- What is the test task? вҶ’ A standardized task per department. Engineering: "Write a function to reverse a linked list". Research: "Summarize this abstract in 3 bullet points". Product: "Write 3 acceptance criteria for a login feature". The test is always the same for a given department so scores are comparable.
- How long does the interview take? вҶ’ Each candidate gets a 60-second timeout. Total: ~3 minutes for 3 candidates (run in parallel).
- Can the Chairman define a custom test task? вҶ’ Not in v1. Standardized tests only.

---

## Requirements

### Functional Requirements

- **FR-001**: A "Smart Hire" button MUST be accessible from the Agents tab
- **FR-002**: The input MUST accept a free-text natural language description (no form fields)
- **FR-003**: The system MUST generate exactly 3 candidate profiles per hire request
- **FR-004**: Each candidate MUST receive the same standardized test task for their inferred department
- **FR-005**: Candidates MUST run in parallel (not sequentially) to minimize wait time
- **FR-006**: Each candidate run MUST be time-bounded (default: 60 seconds timeout)
- **FR-007**: Scoring MUST be done by an LLM evaluator (not regex/heuristics) using a fixed rubric
- **FR-008**: The winning candidate MUST be automatically hired via the existing `POST /api/hire` route
- **FR-009**: Interview events MUST be broadcast via WebSocket and appear in the Live Log
- **FR-010**: Interview results (profiles, scores, test outputs) MUST be stored in `archive/interviews/{id}.json`

### Scoring Rubric (for LLM evaluator)

Each candidate is scored 0вҖ“10 on four dimensions:

| Dimension | Weight | Description |
|---|---|---|
| Task Completion | 40% | Did it actually do what was asked? |
| Output Quality | 30% | Is the output correct, precise, useful? |
| Autonomy | 20% | Did it make decisions without asking questions? |
| Conciseness | 10% | Did it avoid unnecessary verbosity? |

Final score = weighted average. Tiebreak: simpler system prompt wins.

### Candidate Generation Prompt (meta-prompt sent to Claude)

```
You are a hiring manager at ClawCorp, an autonomous AI organization.

The Chairman needs: "{description}"

Generate 3 distinct agent profiles in JSON array format. Each should have:
- A different specialization angle or prompting strategy
- A system_prompt that is specific, actionable, and includes the 3 mandatory rules:
  1. Never ask clarifying questions
  2. Use escalate tool if blocked
  3. Use report tool when done

Infer the correct department and reports_to from the description.
Return only a JSON array, no explanation.
```

### Standardized Test Tasks (per department)

```
Engineering:   "Write a TypeScript function isPalindrome(s: string): boolean with 3 test cases"
Research Lab:  "List 5 open research questions in the field of: {topic inferred from description}"
Product:       "Write 3 Given/When/Then acceptance scenarios for a user login feature"
```

---

## Success Criteria

- **SC-001**: Chairman can go from description to hired agent in under 5 minutes
- **SC-002**: The hired agent's system_prompt is more specific and relevant than what a human would write in a hire form
- **SC-003**: All 3 candidates run in parallel вҖ” total interview time вүӨ 3Г— single candidate time
- **SC-004**: The Chairman can always see what happened (no black box) via Live Log
- **SC-005**: Rejected candidates are archived, not lost вҖ” available for review

Now regarding your follow-up about the org chart for spec 004 вҖ” you'd like a hierarchical tree that goes Chairman вҶ’ CEO/CTO вҶ’ Department groups (e.g. Research Lab as a collapsible unit) вҶ’ team members within each department (leaders, employees, interns). Let me read the current spec 004 and update it.

content.content:
1вҶ’# Feature Specification: Agent Hierarchy & Team Structure
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’**Feature Branch**: `004-hierarchy`
     4вҶ’**Created**: 2026-02-22
     5вҶ’**Status**: Draft
     6вҶ’**Authors**: Chairman + Claude
     7вҶ’
     8вҶ’---
     9вҶ’
    10вҶ’## Context
    11вҶ’
    12вҶ’ClawCorp currently stores a flat list of agents with `reports_to` and `subordinates` fields, but there is no formal concept of a **team**. The Agents tab shows agents as a grid of cards with no visual hierarchy. Cross-agent delegation is individual-to-individual with no team boundary.
    13вҶ’
    14вҶ’This spec introduces:
    15вҶ’1. **Teams** as first-class entities вҖ” dynamic, chairman-created, extensible
    16вҶ’2. **Hierarchical Agents view** вҖ” org chart showing team structure
    17вҶ’3. **Cross-team collaboration** вҖ” structured task + context passing between team leads
    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’The key constraint: **teams are dynamic**. The Chairman can add new teams at any time. No team names or structures are hardcoded.
    20вҶ’
    21вҶ’---
    22вҶ’
    23вҶ’## Architecture
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’### Team as a first-class entity
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’A team is a named group with one **lead** and any number of **members**. The lead is the external interface of the team вҖ” all cross-team tasks enter and exit through the lead.
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’```
    30вҶ’Chairman
    31вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ Research Team          вҶҗ team entity
    32вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Lead: Principal Investigator
    33вҶ’в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Members: Research Assistant, ...
    34вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ Engineering Team       вҶҗ team entity
    35вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Lead: Product Manager
    36вҶ’в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Members: Architect, Senior Engineer, QA Engineer, Intern, ...
    37вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җ [New Team]             вҶҗ Chairman can add at any time
    38вҶ’    в”ңв”Җв”Җ Lead: [agent]
    39вҶ’    в””в”Җв”Җ Members: [agents]
    40вҶ’```
    41вҶ’
    42вҶ’### Communication rules
    43вҶ’
    44вҶ’- **Within a team**: members report to lead; lead reports to Chairman
    45вҶ’- **Cross-team**: Lead вҶ’ Lead only. Team members do NOT directly contact agents in other teams
    46вҶ’- **Escalation**: always goes up within the team first, then to Chairman if the lead is also blocked
    47вҶ’- **Chairman**: passive observer; only contacted when all leads are blocked
    48вҶ’
    49вҶ’### Context packet (cross-team handoff)
    50вҶ’
    51вҶ’When a lead passes a task to another team's lead, a **context packet** travels with it:
    52вҶ’
    53вҶ’```json
    54вҶ’{
    55вҶ’  "from_team": "engineering",
    56вҶ’  "to_team": "research",
    57вҶ’  "task": "...",
    58вҶ’  "why": "We need this to complete the auth module",
    59вҶ’  "known": "JWT is the chosen approach, user table schema is attached",
    60вҶ’  "expected_output": "A security analysis report in markdown"
    61вҶ’}
    62вҶ’```
    63вҶ’
    64вҶ’---
    65вҶ’
    66вҶ’## User Scenarios & Testing
    67вҶ’
    68вҶ’### User Story 1 вҖ” View the org chart (Priority: P1)
    69вҶ’
    70вҶ’As Chairman, I want to see the entire organization as a hierarchy tree so I can understand who reports to whom and which team is doing what.
    71вҶ’
    72вҶ’**Why this priority**: This is the primary visualization goal of the feature.
    73вҶ’
    74вҶ’**Independent Test**: Open the Agents tab. See a tree layout with Chairman at top, teams as branches, leads below Chairman, members below leads. Running agents show a green dot.
    75вҶ’
    76вҶ’**Acceptance Scenarios**:
    77вҶ’
    78вҶ’1. **Given** 3 teams exist (Research, Engineering, a new custom team), **When** I open the Agents tab, **Then** I see a tree with 3 branches under Chairman, each showing the team name and lead
    79вҶ’2. **Given** an agent is currently running, **When** I view the org chart, **Then** that agent's node shows a live green dot
    80вҶ’3. **Given** I add a new team via the UI, **When** I return to the org chart, **Then** the new team appears as a new branch without page reload
    81вҶ’4. **Given** an agent has no team assigned, **When** I view the org chart, **Then** it appears in an "Unassigned" section below the tree (not lost)
    82вҶ’
    83вҶ’---
    84вҶ’
    85вҶ’### User Story 2 вҖ” Create and manage teams (Priority: P1)
    86вҶ’
    87вҶ’As Chairman, I want to create a new team by giving it a name and assigning a lead agent, so the org can grow without any hardcoded limits.
    88вҶ’
    89вҶ’**Why this priority**: The entire feature is built on dynamic teams. Without this, hierarchy is static.
    90вҶ’
    91вҶ’**Independent Test**: Click "New Team", enter name "Data Team", assign lead "data-analyst", confirm. The org chart gains a new branch.
    92вҶ’
    93вҶ’**Acceptance Scenarios**:
    94вҶ’
    95вҶ’1. **Given** I click "New Team" and fill in name + lead, **When** I confirm, **Then** a new team appears in the org chart immediately
    96вҶ’2. **Given** a team exists, **When** I add an agent to it as a member, **Then** that agent appears as a child of the team lead in the org chart
    97вҶ’3. **Given** a team has a lead, **When** I change the lead to a different agent, **Then** the old lead becomes a regular member and the new lead is elevated
    98вҶ’4. **Given** I delete a team, **When** confirmed, **Then** member agents become "Unassigned" (not deleted) and the team branch is removed
    99вҶ’
   100вҶ’---
   101вҶ’
   102вҶ’### User Story 3 вҖ” Cross-team task delegation with context (Priority: P2)
   103вҶ’
   104вҶ’As a team lead agent, I want to pass a task to another team's lead with a structured context packet, so the receiving team understands not just *what* to do but *why* and *what's already known*.
   105вҶ’
   106вҶ’**Why this priority**: Without context, cross-team handoffs lose information and produce misaligned results.
   107вҶ’
   108вҶ’**Independent Test**: Engineering Lead delegates to Research Lead via MCP `cross_team_delegate` tool. Research Lead receives task + context packet. Engineering Lead gets a result back when Research Team is done.
   109вҶ’
   110вҶ’**Acceptance Scenarios**:
   111вҶ’
   112вҶ’1. **Given** PM (Engineering lead) calls `cross_team_delegate` with task + why + known + expected_output, **Then** PI (Research lead) receives a mission with all four fields visible in the mission context
   113вҶ’2. **Given** Research Team completes the delegated task, **When** PI calls `report`, **Then** the result is delivered back to PM's mission (not just to Chairman inbox)
   114вҶ’3. **Given** a cross-team task is in flight, **When** I view the Inbox, **Then** I can see the cross-team handoff as a distinct event showing from-team вҶ’ to-team
   115вҶ’4. **Given** the receiving lead is busy (agent running), **When** a cross-team delegate arrives, **Then** it queues and the lead picks it up on their next run
   116вҶ’
   117вҶ’---
   118вҶ’
   119вҶ’### User Story 4 вҖ” Hire an agent directly into a team (Priority: P2)
   120вҶ’
   121вҶ’As Chairman, when hiring a new agent (manually or via Smart Hire), I want to assign them to a team immediately so they appear in the org chart in the right place.
   122вҶ’
   123вҶ’**Why this priority**: Without this, hired agents land in "Unassigned" and must be manually placed.
   124вҶ’
   125вҶ’**Independent Test**: In the Hire modal, there is a "Team" dropdown. Select "Research Team". New agent appears under Research Team in org chart.
   126вҶ’
   127вҶ’**Acceptance Scenarios**:
   128вҶ’
   129вҶ’1. **Given** I hire a new agent and select "Research Team", **When** hire completes, **Then** the agent appears as a member under PI in the org chart
   130вҶ’2. **Given** Smart Hire infers the department from the description, **When** hire completes, **Then** the agent is automatically placed in the matching team
   131вҶ’3. **Given** no matching team exists for the inferred department, **When** hired, **Then** the agent is placed in "Unassigned"
   132вҶ’
   133вҶ’---
   134вҶ’
   135вҶ’### Edge Cases
   136вҶ’
   137вҶ’- What if a lead agent is fired? вҶ’ Team still exists; needs a new lead assigned before it can function. Show warning in org chart: "Team has no lead"
   138вҶ’- What if a member is assigned to two teams? вҶ’ Not allowed. One agent = one team. Enforce on write.
   139вҶ’- What if `reports_to` in `profile.json` contradicts team membership? вҶ’ Team membership wins. `reports_to` is derived from team structure, not the other way around.
   140вҶ’- What is the maximum team size? вҶ’ No enforced limit.
   141вҶ’- Can Chairman be a team lead? вҶ’ No. Chairman is above all teams.
   142вҶ’- Can a team lead be a member of another team? вҶ’ No. Lead role is exclusive.
   143вҶ’- What if cross-team delegate target lead is in a running mission? вҶ’ Queue the task as a pending message. Lead processes it on next available run.
   144вҶ’
   145вҶ’---
   146вҶ’
   147вҶ’## Requirements
   148вҶ’
   149вҶ’### Data Model
   150вҶ’
   151вҶ’**Team schema** (`teams/{id}/team.json`):
   152вҶ’```json
   153вҶ’{
   154вҶ’  "id": "research-team",
   155вҶ’  "name": "Research Team",
   156вҶ’  "lead": "principal-investigator",
   157вҶ’  "members": ["research-assistant"],
   158вҶ’  "created_at": "ISO"
   159вҶ’}
   160вҶ’```
   161вҶ’
   162вҶ’**Agent profile update** вҖ” add `team` field:
   163вҶ’```json
   164вҶ’{
   165вҶ’  "id": "research-assistant",
   166вҶ’  "team": "research-team",
   167вҶ’  "reports_to": "principal-investigator"
   168вҶ’}
   169вҶ’```
   170вҶ’
   171вҶ’`reports_to` is now **derived** from team structure (always = team lead), kept for backwards compatibility.
   172вҶ’
   173вҶ’### New MCP Tool: `cross_team_delegate`
   174вҶ’
   175вҶ’```
   176вҶ’cross_team_delegate(
   177вҶ’  to_team: string,          // team ID
   178вҶ’  task: string,             // what to do
   179вҶ’  why: string,              // why this is needed
   180вҶ’  known: string,            // relevant context / prior work
   181вҶ’  expected_output: string   // format or type of result expected
   182вҶ’) вҶ’ { handoff_id: string }
   183вҶ’```
   184вҶ’
   185вҶ’Only callable by agents who are team leads. Non-leads get an error: "Only team leads can delegate cross-team."
   186вҶ’
   187вҶ’### Functional Requirements
   188вҶ’
   189вҶ’- **FR-001**: Teams MUST be stored as files in `teams/{id}/team.json` (dynamic, not hardcoded)
   190вҶ’- **FR-002**: The Agents tab MUST render a tree layout (org chart) as the primary view
   191вҶ’- **FR-003**: Each team node MUST show: team name, lead name, member count, running status
   192вҶ’- **FR-004**: The tree MUST update in real time via WebSocket when agent status changes
   193вҶ’- **FR-005**: `GET /api/teams` MUST return all teams with their leads and members resolved
   194вҶ’- **FR-006**: `POST /api/teams` MUST create a new team (name + lead required)
   195вҶ’- **FR-007**: `PATCH /api/teams/:id` MUST allow adding/removing members and changing lead
   196вҶ’- **FR-008**: `DELETE /api/teams/:id` MUST move members to "Unassigned", not delete agents
   197вҶ’- **FR-009**: New MCP tool `cross_team_delegate` MUST only be callable by team leads
   198вҶ’- **FR-010**: Cross-team handoffs MUST appear as a distinct event type in the Inbox
   199вҶ’- **FR-011**: Hiring (manual + smart) MUST include a team assignment field
   200вҶ’- **FR-012**: An agent with no team MUST appear in an "Unassigned" section in the org chart
   201вҶ’
   202вҶ’---
   203вҶ’
   204вҶ’## Success Criteria
   205вҶ’
   206вҶ’- **SC-001**: Chairman can read the entire org structure in under 5 seconds by looking at the org chart
   207вҶ’- **SC-002**: Adding a new team takes < 30 seconds (name + lead, confirm)
   208вҶ’- **SC-003**: Cross-team context packet eliminates the "why are we doing this" question вҖ” the receiving lead has all information needed to start immediately
   209вҶ’- **SC-004**: No agent is ever lost вҖ” all agents appear in org chart (tree or Unassigned)
   210вҶ’- **SC-005**: The org chart is fully dynamic вҖ” reflects real state with no page refresh needed
   211вҶ’

# Feature Specification: Agent Hierarchy & Team Structure

**Feature Branch**: `004-hierarchy`
**Created**: 2026-02-22
**Status**: Draft
**Authors**: Chairman + Claude

---

## Context

ClawCorp currently stores a flat list of agents with `reports_to` and `subordinates` fields, but there is no formal concept of a **team**. The Agents tab shows agents as a grid of cards with no visual hierarchy. Cross-agent delegation is individual-to-individual with no team boundary.

This spec introduces:
1. **Teams** as first-class entities вҖ” dynamic, chairman-created, extensible
2. **Hierarchical Agents view** вҖ” org chart showing team structure
3. **Cross-team collaboration** вҖ” structured task + context passing between team leads

The key constraint: **teams are dynamic**. The Chairman can add new teams at any time. No team names or structures are hardcoded.

---

## Architecture

### Team as a first-class entity

A team is a named group with one **lead** and any number of **members**. The lead is the external interface of the team вҖ” all cross-team tasks enter and exit through the lead.

```
Chairman
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Research Team          вҶҗ team entity
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Lead: Principal Investigator
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Members: Research Assistant, ...
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Engineering Team       вҶҗ team entity
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Lead: Product Manager
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Members: Architect, Senior Engineer, QA Engineer, Intern, ...
в””в”Җв”Җ [New Team]             вҶҗ Chairman can add at any time
    в”ңв”Җв”Җ Lead: [agent]
    в””в”Җв”Җ Members: [agents]
```

### Communication rules

- **Within a team**: members report to lead; lead reports to Chairman
- **Cross-team**: Lead вҶ’ Lead only. Team members do NOT directly contact agents in other teams
- **Escalation**: always goes up within the team first, then to Chairman if the lead is also blocked
- **Chairman**: passive observer; only contacted when all leads are blocked

### Context packet (cross-team handoff)

When a lead passes a task to another team's lead, a **context packet** travels with it:

```json
{
  "from_team": "engineering",
  "to_team": "research",
  "task": "...",
  "why": "We need this to complete the auth module",
  "known": "JWT is the chosen approach, user table schema is attached",
  "expected_output": "A security analysis report in markdown"
}
```

---

## User Scenarios & Testing

### User Story 1 вҖ” View the org chart (Priority: P1)

As Chairman, I want to see the entire organization as a hierarchy tree so I can understand who reports to whom and which team is doing what.

**Why this priority**: This is the primary visualization goal of the feature.

**Independent Test**: Open the Agents tab. See a tree layout with Chairman at top, teams as branches, leads below Chairman, members below leads. Running agents show a green dot.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** 3 teams exist (Research, Engineering, a new custom team), **When** I open the Agents tab, **Then** I see a tree with 3 branches under Chairman, each showing the team name and lead
2. **Given** an agent is currently running, **When** I view the org chart, **Then** that agent's node shows a live green dot
3. **Given** I add a new team via the UI, **When** I return to the org chart, **Then** the new team appears as a new branch without page reload
4. **Given** an agent has no team assigned, **When** I view the org chart, **Then** it appears in an "Unassigned" section below the tree (not lost)

---

### User Story 2 вҖ” Create and manage teams (Priority: P1)

As Chairman, I want to create a new team by giving it a name and assigning a lead agent, so the org can grow without any hardcoded limits.

**Why this priority**: The entire feature is built on dynamic teams. Without this, hierarchy is static.

**Independent Test**: Click "New Team", enter name "Data Team", assign lead "data-analyst", confirm. The org chart gains a new branch.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** I click "New Team" and fill in name + lead, **When** I confirm, **Then** a new team appears in the org chart immediately
2. **Given** a team exists, **When** I add an agent to it as a member, **Then** that agent appears as a child of the team lead in the org chart
3. **Given** a team has a lead, **When** I change the lead to a different agent, **Then** the old lead becomes a regular member and the new lead is elevated
4. **Given** I delete a team, **When** confirmed, **Then** member agents become "Unassigned" (not deleted) and the team branch is removed

---

### User Story 3 вҖ” Cross-team task delegation with context (Priority: P2)

As a team lead agent, I want to pass a task to another team's lead with a structured context packet, so the receiving team understands not just *what* to do but *why* and *what's already known*.

**Why this priority**: Without context, cross-team handoffs lose information and produce misaligned results.

**Independent Test**: Engineering Lead delegates to Research Lead via MCP `cross_team_delegate` tool. Research Lead receives task + context packet. Engineering Lead gets a result back when Research Team is done.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** PM (Engineering lead) calls `cross_team_delegate` with task + why + known + expected_output, **Then** PI (Research lead) receives a mission with all four fields visible in the mission context
2. **Given** Research Team completes the delegated task, **When** PI calls `report`, **Then** the result is delivered back to PM's mission (not just to Chairman inbox)
3. **Given** a cross-team task is in flight, **When** I view the Inbox, **Then** I can see the cross-team handoff as a distinct event showing from-team вҶ’ to-team
4. **Given** the receiving lead is busy (agent running), **When** a cross-team delegate arrives, **Then** it queues and the lead picks it up on their next run

---

### User Story 4 вҖ” Hire an agent directly into a team (Priority: P2)

As Chairman, when hiring a new agent (manually or via Smart Hire), I want to assign them to a team immediately so they appear in the org chart in the right place.

**Why this priority**: Without this, hired agents land in "Unassigned" and must be manually placed.

**Independent Test**: In the Hire modal, there is a "Team" dropdown. Select "Research Team". New agent appears under Research Team in org chart.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** I hire a new agent and select "Research Team", **When** hire completes, **Then** the agent appears as a member under PI in the org chart
2. **Given** Smart Hire infers the department from the description, **When** hire completes, **Then** the agent is automatically placed in the matching team
3. **Given** no matching team exists for the inferred department, **When** hired, **Then** the agent is placed in "Unassigned"

---

### Edge Cases

- What if a lead agent is fired? вҶ’ Team still exists; needs a new lead assigned before it can function. Show warning in org chart: "Team has no lead"
- What if a member is assigned to two teams? вҶ’ Not allowed. One agent = one team. Enforce on write.
- What if `reports_to` in `profile.json` contradicts team membership? вҶ’ Team membership wins. `reports_to` is derived from team structure, not the other way around.
- What is the maximum team size? вҶ’ No enforced limit.
- Can Chairman be a team lead? вҶ’ No. Chairman is above all teams.
- Can a team lead be a member of another team? вҶ’ No. Lead role is exclusive.
- What if cross-team delegate target lead is in a running mission? вҶ’ Queue the task as a pending message. Lead processes it on next available run.

---

## Requirements

### Data Model

**Team schema** (`teams/{id}/team.json`):
```json
{
  "id": "research-team",
  "name": "Research Team",
  "lead": "principal-investigator",
  "members": ["research-assistant"],
  "created_at": "ISO"
}
```

**Agent profile update** вҖ” add `team` field:
```json
{
  "id": "research-assistant",
  "team": "research-team",
  "reports_to": "principal-investigator"
}
```

`reports_to` is now **derived** from team structure (always = team lead), kept for backwards compatibility.

### New MCP Tool: `cross_team_delegate`

```
cross_team_delegate(
  to_team: string,          // team ID
  task: string,             // what to do
  why: string,              // why this is needed
  known: string,            // relevant context / prior work
  expected_output: string   // format or type of result expected
) вҶ’ { handoff_id: string }
```

Only callable by agents who are team leads. Non-leads get an error: "Only team leads can delegate cross-team."

### Functional Requirements

- **FR-001**: Teams MUST be stored as files in `teams/{id}/team.json` (dynamic, not hardcoded)
- **FR-002**: The Agents tab MUST render a tree layout (org chart) as the primary view
- **FR-003**: Each team node MUST show: team name, lead name, member count, running status
- **FR-004**: The tree MUST update in real time via WebSocket when agent status changes
- **FR-005**: `GET /api/teams` MUST return all teams with their leads and members resolved
- **FR-006**: `POST /api/teams` MUST create a new team (name + lead required)
- **FR-007**: `PATCH /api/teams/:id` MUST allow adding/removing members and changing lead
- **FR-008**: `DELETE /api/teams/:id` MUST move members to "Unassigned", not delete agents
- **FR-009**: New MCP tool `cross_team_delegate` MUST only be callable by team leads
- **FR-010**: Cross-team handoffs MUST appear as a distinct event type in the Inbox
- **FR-011**: Hiring (manual + smart) MUST include a team assignment field
- **FR-012**: An agent with no team MUST appear in an "Unassigned" section in the org chart

---

## Success Criteria

- **SC-001**: Chairman can read the entire org structure in under 5 seconds by looking at the org chart
- **SC-002**: Adding a new team takes < 30 seconds (name + lead, confirm)
- **SC-003**: Cross-team context packet eliminates the "why are we doing this" question вҖ” the receiving lead has all information needed to start immediately
- **SC-004**: No agent is ever lost вҖ” all agents appear in org chart (tree or Unassigned)
- **SC-005**: The org chart is fully dynamic вҖ” reflects real state with no page refresh needed

input.content:
# Feature Specification: Agent Hierarchy & Team Structure

**Feature Branch**: `004-hierarchy`
**Created**: 2026-02-22
**Status**: Draft
**Authors**: Chairman + Claude

---

## Context

ClawCorp currently stores a flat list of agents with `reports_to` and `subordinates` fields. The Agents tab shows an org-chart tree rooted at Chairman, but the tree is flat вҖ” every agent is a direct report or one level deep. There is no formal concept of **executive roles**, **departments as groups**, or **rank levels** within a department.

This spec introduces a **corporate hierarchy** with three layers:

```
Chairman (you)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ CEO
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Engineering          вҶҗ department (clickable)
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ VP of Engineering
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Senior Engineer
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Engineer
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Intern
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ ...
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Product              вҶҗ department (clickable)
в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ Head of Product
в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җ ...
в”ңв”Җв”Җ CTO
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Research Lab          вҶҗ department (clickable)
в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ Principal Investigator
в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Research Scientist
в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Research Intern
в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җ ...
в””в”Җв”Җ [Other C-suite]
```

**Key UX**: The top-level view shows Chairman вҶ’ C-suite вҶ’ Departments as collapsed nodes. Clicking a department expands it into its own internal tree showing the full team hierarchy (leaders, employees, interns).

---

## Architecture

### Three-tier hierarchy

1. **Chairman** вҖ” the user. Root of the tree. Passive observer.
2. **C-suite** вҖ” executive agents (CEO, CTO, COO, etc.) that report directly to Chairman. Each oversees one or more departments.
3. **Departments** вҖ” organizational units (Engineering, Research Lab, Product, etc.). Each department is a **team** with internal hierarchy.

### Agent rank levels

Within a department, agents have a `rank` field:

| Rank | Level | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `executive` | 0 | C-suite (CEO, CTO). Reports to Chairman. |
| `director` | 1 | Department head (VP Engineering, Head of Product). Reports to a C-suite exec. |
| `lead` | 2 | Team lead within a department. Reports to director. |
| `senior` | 3 | Senior individual contributor. Reports to lead or director. |
| `member` | 4 | Regular contributor. Reports to lead or director. |
| `intern` | 5 | Entry-level. Reports to any senior+ agent. |

### Department as a group entity

A department is a named group stored as `teams/{id}/team.json`. It references:
- A **department head** (director-level agent)
- An **executive sponsor** (C-suite agent it reports into)
- A list of **member agent IDs**

The internal tree structure within a department is derived from `reports_to` chains вҖ” not flattened.

### Communication rules

- **Within a department**: agents report up through the chain (intern вҶ’ lead вҶ’ director)
- **Cross-department**: director вҶ’ director only. Lower-rank agents do NOT directly contact other departments
- **To Chairman**: only C-suite executives escalate to Chairman. Lower agents escalate up through the chain
- **Cross-team context**: when a director passes work to another department, a context packet travels with it (same as before)

---

## User Scenarios & Testing

### User Story 1 вҖ” View the corporate org chart (Priority: P1)

As Chairman, I want to see the organization as a multi-level tree: Chairman вҶ’ C-suite вҶ’ Departments (collapsed) вҖ” so I can quickly understand the structure.

**Independent Test**: Open the Agents tab. See Chairman at top, C-suite below, departments as collapsed nodes with member count badges. No need to scroll through every agent.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** 2 C-suite agents (CEO, CTO) and 3 departments, **When** I open the Agents tab, **Then** I see Chairman вҶ’ CEO/CTO вҶ’ their respective departments as labeled nodes
2. **Given** a department node shows "Engineering (4)", **When** I see it, **Then** the "(4)" indicates 4 agents within that department
3. **Given** an agent is running in a department, **When** I view the top-level tree, **Then** that department node shows a green activity dot

---

### User Story 2 вҖ” Drill into a department (Priority: P1)

As Chairman, I want to click on a department (e.g. "Research Lab") to see its internal team tree вҖ” showing the hierarchy from director down to interns.

**Independent Test**: Click "Research Lab" node. The view expands (or navigates) to show the department's internal tree: Principal Investigator вҶ’ Research Scientists вҶ’ Interns.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** I click on "Research Lab", **When** it expands, **Then** I see a tree rooted at the department head (e.g. Principal Investigator), with all members arranged by `reports_to`
2. **Given** a department has 3 levels (director вҶ’ lead вҶ’ intern), **When** expanded, **Then** the tree shows all 3 levels with proper indentation
3. **Given** I click the department again (or a "collapse" button), **When** it collapses, **Then** I return to the top-level view
4. **Given** an agent within the department is running, **When** I view the expanded tree, **Then** that agent shows a green dot and running status

---

### User Story 3 вҖ” Create and manage teams (Priority: P1)

As Chairman, I want to create a new department, assign an executive sponsor and department head, and add members.

**Independent Test**: Click "New Department", enter name "Data Science", assign executive sponsor "CTO", assign department head, confirm. A new department node appears.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** I click "New Department" and fill in name + executive sponsor + department head, **When** I confirm, **Then** a new department node appears under the chosen executive
2. **Given** a department exists, **When** I add an agent to it, **Then** that agent appears within the department's internal tree
3. **Given** I want to restructure, **When** I move an agent between departments, **Then** their `reports_to` updates and they appear in the new department
4. **Given** I delete a department, **When** confirmed, **Then** member agents become "Unassigned" (not deleted)

---

### User Story 4 вҖ” Cross-department delegation with context (Priority: P2)

As a department head, I want to pass a task to another department's head with structured context, so the receiving department understands what to do, why, and what's already known.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** Engineering director calls `cross_team_delegate` with task + context, **Then** Research director receives the task with full context
2. **Given** a cross-department task completes, **When** the research director reports, **Then** the result goes back to the engineering director (not Chairman)
3. **Given** a cross-department handoff is in flight, **When** I view the Inbox, **Then** I see it as a distinct event showing departmentвҶ’department

---

### User Story 5 вҖ” Hire into the hierarchy (Priority: P2)

As Chairman, when hiring a new agent (manually or Smart Hire), I want to place them in a department at the right rank level.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** Smart Hire infers department "Engineering" and rank "member", **When** hired, **Then** the agent appears in Engineering under the correct lead
2. **Given** I hire a C-suite agent (e.g. COO), **When** hired, **Then** they appear as a direct report of Chairman at the top level

---

### Edge Cases

- What if a C-suite agent is fired? вҶ’ Their departments become "unsponsored" вҖ” show warning. Chairman must reassign.
- What if a department head is fired? вҶ’ Department still exists but shows "No Head" warning. Chairman must assign a new one.
- What if an agent's `reports_to` points to someone outside their department? вҶ’ Department membership wins. `reports_to` is corrected to the department head.
- Can an agent be in multiple departments? вҶ’ No. One agent = one department.
- Can Chairman be a department head? вҶ’ No. Chairman is above all departments and executives.
- Maximum depth of hierarchy within a department? вҶ’ No enforced limit, but typical is 2-3 levels.

---

## Requirements

### Data Model

**Team/Department schema** (`teams/{id}/team.json`):
```json
{
  "id": "engineering",
  "name": "Engineering",
  "executive_sponsor": "ceo",
  "head": "vp-engineering",
  "members": ["senior-engineer", "engineer-1", "intern-1"],
  "created_at": "ISO"
}
```

**Agent profile update** вҖ” add `rank` and `team` fields:
```json
{
  "id": "senior-engineer",
  "rank": "senior",
  "team": "engineering",
  "reports_to": "vp-engineering"
}
```

### UI Layout

**Top-level view (collapsed)**:
```
Chairman в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ [You]
в”ңв”Җв”Җ CEO в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ executive
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Engineering (4) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ [click to expand]
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Product (2) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ [click to expand]
в””в”Җв”Җ CTO в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ executive
    в””в”Җв”Җ Research Lab (3) в”Җв”Җв”Җ [click to expand]
```

**Expanded department view** (e.g. click "Engineering"):
```
вҶҗ Back to Org Chart

Engineering
в”ңв”Җв”Җ VP of Engineering в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ director
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Senior Engineer в”Җв”Җ senior
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Engineer в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ member
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Intern в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ intern
```

### MCP Tool: `cross_team_delegate`

```
cross_team_delegate(
  to_team: string,
  task: string,
  why: string,
  known: string,
  expected_output: string
) вҶ’ { handoff_id: string }
```

Only callable by agents with rank `director` or `executive`. Others get an error.

### Functional Requirements

- **FR-001**: Teams/departments MUST be stored in `teams/{id}/team.json`
- **FR-002**: The Agents tab MUST show a top-level tree: Chairman вҶ’ C-suite вҶ’ Departments (collapsed)
- **FR-003**: Clicking a department MUST expand/navigate to show its internal hierarchy tree
- **FR-004**: Each department node MUST show: name, member count, running status indicator
- **FR-005**: Each agent node MUST show: title, rank badge, running status
- **FR-006**: `GET /api/teams` MUST return all departments with resolved members and hierarchy
- **FR-007**: `POST /api/teams` MUST create a new department (name + executive sponsor + head required)
- **FR-008**: `PATCH /api/teams/:id` MUST allow adding/removing members and changing head
- **FR-009**: `DELETE /api/teams/:id` MUST move members to "Unassigned", not delete agents
- **FR-010**: `cross_team_delegate` MUST only be callable by director+ rank agents
- **FR-011**: Cross-department handoffs MUST appear as distinct events in Inbox
- **FR-012**: Hiring MUST include department + rank assignment (inferred by Smart Hire, manual for regular hire)
- **FR-013**: Agents with no department MUST appear in "Unassigned" section
- **FR-014**: The tree MUST update in real time via WebSocket

---

## Success Criteria

- **SC-001**: Chairman can see the full org structure at a glance in < 3 seconds (collapsed view)
- **SC-002**: Drilling into a department to see team details takes one click
- **SC-003**: Adding a new department takes < 30 seconds
- **SC-004**: No agent is ever lost вҖ” all appear in tree or Unassigned
- **SC-005**: Org chart is fully dynamic вҖ” no page refresh needed

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** 2 C-suite agents (CEO, CTO) and 3 departments, **When** I open the Agents tab, **Then** I see Chairman вҶ’ CEO/CTO вҶ’ their respective departments as labeled nodes
2. **Given** a department node shows "Engineering (4)", **When** I see it, **Then** the "(4)" indicates 4 agents within that department
3. **Given** an agent is running in a department, **When** I view the top-level tree, **Then** that department node shows a green activity dot

---

### User Story 2 вҖ” Drill into a department (Priority: P1)

As Chairman, I want to click on a department (e.g. "Research Lab") to see its internal team tree вҖ” showing the hierarchy from director down to interns.

**Independent Test**: Click "Research Lab" node. The view expands (or navigates) to show the department's internal tree: Principal Investigator вҶ’ Research Scientists вҶ’ Interns.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** I click on "Research Lab", **When** it expands, **Then** I see a tree rooted at the department head (e.g. Principal Investigator), with all members arranged by `reports_to`
2. **Given** a department has 3 levels (director вҶ’ lead вҶ’ intern), **When** expanded, **Then** the tree shows all 3 levels with proper indentation
3. **Given** I click the department again (or a "collapse" button), **When** it collapses, **Then** I return to the top-level view
4. **Given** an agent within the department is running, **When** I view the expanded tree, **Then** that agent shows a green dot and running status

---

### User Story 3 вҖ” Create and manage teams (Priority: P1)

As Chairman, I want to create a new department, assign an executive sponsor and department head, and add members.

**Independent Test**: Click "New Department", enter name "Data Science", assign executive sponsor "CTO", assign department head, confirm. A new department node appears.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** I click "New Department" and fill in name + executive sponsor + department head, **When** I confirm, **Then** a new department node appears under the chosen executive
2. **Given** a department exists, **When** I add an agent to it, **Then** that agent appears within the department's internal tree
3. **Given** I want to restructure, **When** I move an agent between departments, **Then** their `reports_to` updates and they appear in the new department
4. **Given** I delete a department, **When** confirmed, **Then** member agents become "Unassigned" (not deleted)

---

### User Story 4 вҖ” Cross-department delegation with context (Priority: P2)

As a department head, I want to pass a task to another department's head with structured context, so the receiving department understands what to do, why, and what's already known.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** Engineering director calls `cross_team_delegate` with task + context, **Then** Research director receives the task with full context
2. **Given** a cross-department task completes, **When** the research director reports, **Then** the result goes back to the engineering director (not Chairman)
3. **Given** a cross-department handoff is in flight, **When** I view the Inbox, **Then** I see it as a distinct event showing departmentвҶ’department

---

### User Story 5 вҖ” Hire into the hierarchy (Priority: P2)

As Chairman, when hiring a new agent (manually or Smart Hire), I want to place them in a department at the right rank level.

**Acceptance Scenarios**:

1. **Given** Smart Hire infers department "Engineering" and rank "member", **When** hired, **Then** the agent appears in Engineering under the correct lead
2. **Given** I hire a C-suite agent (e.g. COO), **When** hired, **Then** they appear as a direct report of Chairman at the top level

---

### Edge Cases

- What if a C-suite agent is fired? вҶ’ Their departments become "unsponsored" вҖ” show warning. Chairman must reassign.
- What if a department head is fired? вҶ’ Department still exists but shows "No Head" warning. Chairman must assign a new one.
- What if an agent's `reports_to` points to someone outside their department? вҶ’ Department membership wins. `reports_to` is corrected to the department head.
- Can an agent be in multiple departments? вҶ’ No. One agent = one department.
- Can Chairman be a department head? вҶ’ No. Chairman is above all departments and executives.
- Maximum depth of hierarchy within a department? вҶ’ No enforced limit, but typical is 2-3 levels.

---

## Requirements

### Data Model

**Team/Department schema** (`teams/{id}/team.json`):
```json
{
  "id": "engineering",
  "name": "Engineering",
  "executive_sponsor": "ceo",
  "head": "vp-engineering",
  "members": ["senior-engineer", "engineer-1", "intern-1"],
  "created_at": "ISO"
}
```

**Agent profile update** вҖ” add `rank` and `team` fields:
```json
{
  "id": "senior-engineer",
  "rank": "senior",
  "team": "engineering",
  "reports_to": "vp-engineering"
}
```

### UI Layout

**Top-level view (collapsed)**:
```
Chairman в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ [You]
в”ңв”Җв”Җ CEO в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ executive
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Engineering (4) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ [click to expand]
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Product (2) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ [click to expand]
в””в”Җв”Җ CTO в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ executive
    в””в”Җв”Җ Research Lab (3) в”Җв”Җв”Җ [click to expand]
```

**Expanded department view** (e.g. click "Engineering"):
```
вҶҗ Back to Org Chart

Engineering
в”ңв”Җв”Җ VP of Engineering в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ director
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Senior Engineer в”Җв”Җ senior
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Engineer в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ member
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Intern в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ intern
```

### MCP Tool: `cross_team_delegate`

```
cross_team_delegate(
  to_team: string,
  task: string,
  why: string,
  known: string,
  expected_output: string
) вҶ’ { handoff_id: string }
```

Only callable by agents with rank `director` or `executive`. Others get an error.

### Functional Requirements

- **FR-001**: Teams/departments MUST be stored in `teams/{id}/team.json`
- **FR-002**: The Agents tab MUST show a top-level tree: Chairman вҶ’ C-suite вҶ’ Departments (collapsed)
- **FR-003**: Clicking a department MUST expand/navigate to show its internal hierarchy tree
- **FR-004**: Each department node MUST show: name, member count, running status indicator
- **FR-005**: Each agent node MUST show: title, rank badge, running status
- **FR-006**: `GET /api/teams` MUST return all departments with resolved members and hierarchy
- **FR-007**: `POST /api/teams` MUST create a new department (name + executive sponsor + head required)
- **FR-008**: `PATCH /api/teams/:id` MUST allow adding/removing members and changing head
- **FR-009**: `DELETE /api/teams/:id` MUST move members to "Unassigned", not delete agents
- **FR-010**: `cross_team_delegate` MUST only be callable by director+ rank agents
- **FR-011**: Cross-department handoffs MUST appear as distinct events in Inbox
- **FR-012**: Hiring MUST include department + rank assignment (inferred by Smart Hire, manual for regular hire)
- **FR-013**: Agents with no department MUST appear in "Unassigned" section
- **FR-014**: The tree MUST update in real time via WebSocket

---

## Success Criteria

- **SC-001**: Chairman can see the full org structure at a glance in < 3 seconds (collapsed view)
- **SC-002**: Drilling into a department to see team details takes one click
- **SC-003**: Adding a new department takes < 30 seconds
- **SC-004**: No agent is ever lost вҖ” all appear in tree or Unassigned
- **SC-005**: Org chart is fully dynamic вҖ” no page refresh needed
